_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identifying the developmental needs of others and coaching, mentoring, or otherwise helping others to improve their knowledge or skills.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in mentoring and developing data and AI professionals, including individualized development planning, technical coaching, career guidance, and creating growth opportunities within the organizational context.
comments: Excellent match. WIDAI LS-S-007 is a direct, specific expression of coaching and development for data/AI professionals. Also: AB-S-004 (mentoring junior analysts). Very strong coverage.
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score for an Equal relationship reflects vocabulary divergence between O*NET's general-purpose language and WIDAI's domain-specific terminology. The concepts are equivalent despite surface-level phrasing differences that compress the cross-encoder score.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8754 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. Balanced P/R is consistent with the Equal classification — both descriptions cover each other's semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Entailment-dominant (96.03%) — the model detects that one description logically implies aspects of the other. Strong entailment signal supports conceptual equivalence between these descriptions.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.6582) meets or exceeds the STS cross-encoder (0.6333). This is uncommon and suggests the semantic similarity is well-represented in the embedding space — the descriptions occupy similar vector-space regions even without joint processing.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (11.11% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
